australian_relation: ABC Curious Hobart page with separately bounded, named ghost-person accounts at verified Hobart sites.
coordinate_evidence_note: ABC page names the Drunken Admiral on Hobart's waterfront; coordinates use the state-checked OSM/Nominatim Drunken Admiral point.
evidence_summary: The Drunken Admiral sits on Hobart's waterfront. A customer from Canada said she had a number of experiences while dining at the restaurant. She said there was a gentleman with a grey beard who was very upset about what was happening at the restaurant. Jacques Imbriotis said he went home, researched the report, and identified the apparition as Henry Jones himself.
publication_or_organisation: What are Hobart's most famous ghost stories?; Australian Broadcasting Corporation
title: Henry Jones in Drunken Admiral Henry Jones apparition

australian_relation: ABC Curious Hobart page with separately bounded, named ghost-person accounts at verified Hobart sites.
coordinate_evidence_note: ABC page names the Lenna of Hobart Hotel; coordinates use the state-checked OSM/Nominatim Lenna of Hobart point.
evidence_summary: The Lenna of Hobart Hotel was built in 1873 and owned by ship builder and merchant Alexander McGregor. Mr McGregor died in 1896 at his home, and some believe he still haunts the now-hotel with his wife Harriet. Jacques Imbriotis said Alex still hangs around there and treats it like his castle on top the hill, despite being dead for over a century.
publication_or_organisation: What are Hobart's most famous ghost stories?; Australian Broadcasting Corporation

raw_metadata_json.chapter: Lenna of Hobart Alex and Harriet

title: Alex in Lenna of Hobart Alex and Harriet

australian_relation: ABC Curious Hobart page with separately bounded, named ghost-person accounts at verified Hobart sites.
coordinate_evidence_note: ABC page names Narryna in Battery Point; coordinates use the state-checked OSM/Nominatim Narryna Heritage Museum point.
evidence_summary: Narryna in Battery Point is an 1830s merchant's house that now operates as a museum. It apparently has the area's longest-serving ghost, a young convict girl named Polly. Jacques Imbriotis said Polly was a young convict staff member who died when she was pregnant, either pushed or fallen down the stairs.
publication_or_organisation: What are Hobart's most famous ghost stories?; Australian Broadcasting Corporation
title: Polly in Narryna Polly ghost
